description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body that bulges outward at the center and tapers toward the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each tightly bound together by three prominent black metal hoops—one at the top, one at the bottom, and one around the middle. The wood has a weathered, medium-brown finish, showing subtle grain patterns and a slightly rough texture. The top of the barrel is closed with a flat, circular wooden lid, seamlessly integrated with the rest of the structure. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings, giving it a utilitarian and traditional appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near shops and taverns.
description: A traditional wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a closed wooden lid, featuring a weathered brown finish and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from the front, showing the full curvature of the staves and the three black metal hoops encircling the body.
description_view_1: This view highlights the same features as the front, with the wood grain and metal hoops clearly visible, and no additional details or openings.
description_view_2: The barrel's side is shown, maintaining the consistent appearance of the wood and metal bands, with no handles or markings.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to the others, emphasizing the uniformity of the wood staves and the secure placement of the metal hoops.
